response: solution The erroneous code likely fails because it does not handle duplicate values correctly when reordering, causing adjacent equal elements in cases like [1,1,1,4,5,6]. solution A robust approach involves sorting the array and then placing elements by interleaving the first half and second half in reverse order to avoid adjacent duplicates. solution For the O(n) follow-up without full sorting, we can use the QuickSelect algorithm to find the median and then partition the array into smaller and larger halves before applying the same interleaving logic. solution This method works for both positive and negative integers because it relies only on relative comparisons, not on the sign or magnitude of the values. solution The final implementation ensures that no two adjacent elements are equal whenever a valid arrangement exists, satisfying all constraints.
